description: Configuration authority model defining what each layer is allowed to contain. Used by config-authority-guard.sh.

global.description: Machine/provider/auth layer only. Provider auth, Doppler/env references, machine-local plugin/auth plumbing, fallback provider.
global.notes: After C4 migration, global should NOT contain agent definitions, model routing, MCP policy, or behavioral config.

workspace.description: Behavioral runtime authority. Agents, model routing, MCP policy, permissions, compaction, default agent, prompts.
workspace.notes: After C3 migration, workspace MUST declare all agents, models, MCPs, and permissions.

brain_config.description: Orchestration/routing/eval policy authority. Higher-level protocol intelligence.
brain_config.notes: Must align with workspace opencode.json on default_model, small_model, and helper roster.

repo.description: Exception-only. Hooks, local tools, explicit overrides.
repo.exception_process: To add non-hook content to a repo .opencode/, create an ADR in .opencode/adr/ with explicit approval.
repo.notes: Repos inherit workspace behavior by default. No repo-level agent/model/MCP declarations without ADR.

enforcement.switch_condition: After C4 migration complete and all guards pass in audit mode
